findings.evidence: PR line 169: `theorem le_comap_map {A D : Subcomodule R C M} (hAD : A ≤ D) ... : A ≤ (D.map f).comap f`; Mathlib precedent uses `le_comap_map` for the same object on both sides, e.g. `Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Map.lean:465: theorem le_comap_map (H : Subgroup G) : H ≤ comap f (map f H)`.
findings.fix: Rename it to `le_comap_map_of_le` or specialize it to the standard `D ≤ (D.map f).comap f` statement and add the generalized version under an `_of_le` name.
findings.issue: `le_comap_map` is the standard name for `D ≤ (D.map f).comap f`, but this theorem states the generalized `A ≤ (D.map f).comap f` under `hAD : A ≤ D`, so the name hides the required comparison between different subcomodules.

summary: One introduced theorem name is misleading relative to the statement and Mathlib precedent for `le_comap_map`. Rename it to reflect the extra `A ≤ D` hypothesis.
